sections.value: Some of my projects (still work in progress):

items.title: Kurdish Linguistic Variations Map
items.summary: This is the first prototype for an interactive map visualizing dialectal diversity across many Kurdish varieties. Exploring dialectal diversity with data from the Database of Kurdish Dialects (Matras, 2016) and based on the idea by Murat Baran Many more Kurdish NLP Projects will soon appear on Christian's Homepage!

items.title: Language Ontologies & Varieties Explorer (LOVE)
items.summary: Feature Analyses Navigation (FUAN) with Christian Schuler and Yusser Al Ghassin

items.title: Multilingual Text As Corpus Repository
items.summary: Our student research project 'TextAsCorpusRep', a corpus of text data to advance machine translations of low-resource languages. Mentored by Dr. Seid Muhie Yimam got funded by the DDLitLab for up to 10,000€ :D

items.title: Towards a Complete Mapping of Kurdish Dialectology.
items.summary: Together with my dear friends Raman and Anran, I have been working on creating an interactive map of the Kurdish dialects. This map is still very much work-in-progress, but it already seems quite promising! :D

items.title: Analysis of Phonology and Morphology in the Kobani Dialect.
items.summary: Raman and I have been working on analysing the phonology and morphology in the Kobani dialect of the Kurdish language. It has been absolutely amazing, how much we have been learning from each other while working on this project! :D
Our student research project 'StudyToolkitVid', a toolkit to study human perception of video material. Mentored by Prof. Dr. Timo Baumann got funded by the DDLitLab for 10,000€ :D

items.title: Wooden Math
items.summary: In the second semester of studying computer science, two friends and I decided to have a little fun with our math tutor at the time and carved our assignment onto wood, painted it white, and then added black for better readability :D

items.title: Crazy Deck
items.summary: Together with a dear friend (Domi), I designed and created the card game Crazy Deck. This took place over the entire time of us studying computer science at the university of Hamburg and will hopefully continue at some point when we find some free time again! Especially now that Ānrán decided to join us in creating a digital version for online multiplayer! :D Hach... If only the day had more than 24 hours!
